metadata.title: Japan/PMDA-Specific Regulatory Requirements
sources: PMDA Clinical Evaluation (ICH E16 Biomarker Qualification - Japanese Implementation)
PMDA Quality Drug Substances (Foreign Manufacturer Accreditation Guide)
PMDA Stability Testing (ICH Q1-based Japanese Implementation)
PMDA Biologics Quality (ICH E16 - Biomarker Qualification for Biologics)
PMDA Ethnic Factors Bridging (ICH E5 Implementation)

regulatory_framework.governing_law: Pharmaceutical and Medical Device Act (PMD Act, formerly PAL - Pharmaceutical Affairs Law)
regulatory_framework.regulatory_authority: PMDA (Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices Agency) under MHLW (Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare)
regulatory_framework.dossier_format: CTD (Common Technical Document) with Japan-specific Module 1
key_notifications: PSEHB/PED Notification No. 0428-4 (Apr 28, 2021) - Latest foreign manufacturer handling

foreign_manufacturer_accreditation.description: Japan uniquely requires 'Accredited Foreign Manufacturer' status for any foreign entity manufacturing drugs for export to Japan

key_requirements.requirement: Accreditation application to MHLW Minister via PMDA
key_requirements.detail: Form No. 18 (PAL Enforcement Regulations) addressed to Minister + Form No. 16-(2) for PMDA examination
key_requirements.submission_to: Administration Division II, Office of Review Administration, PMDA

key_requirements.detail: Foreign manufacturer must have a Japanese MAH who can apply on their behalf
Form No. 20 for renewal; accreditation becomes null and void if not renewed within effective period
Cannot apply for multiple categories in one application; separate applications required for each manufacturing category
Form No. 6 to PMDA for changes to: person responsible, executives, establishment name, buildings/facilities, category changes
PMDA principally conducts document examination only; on-site inspection of foreign facilities is not standard practice

required_documents: CV of person responsible for manufacturing establishment
Product list for export to Japan with manufacturing process documents
Building/facility documentation (equivalent to domestic manufacturing license docs)
foreign_manufacturer_accreditation.processing_time: Approximately 5 months (based on domestic licensing timeline)
foreign_manufacturer_accreditation.unique_vs_fda_ema: Neither FDA nor EMA requires a separate 'accreditation' step for foreign manufacturers; FDA uses establishment registration + inspection, EMA uses MIA (Manufacturing and Import Authorization)

bridging_study_requirements.ich_e5_framework: Japan was the primary driver of ICH E5 (Ethnic Factors in the Acceptability of Foreign Clinical Data)
bridging_study_requirements.core_principle: Foreign clinical data may be accepted if a bridging study demonstrates similarity of response between Japanese and foreign populations

bridging_study_design.purpose: Demonstrate that foreign clinical data can be extrapolated to the Japanese population
key_factors_evaluated: Intrinsic ethnic factors: genetics, metabolism, body weight, organ function
Extrinsic ethnic factors: diet, medical practice, disease definition, regulatory requirements
Pharmacokinetic differences between Japanese and foreign populations

japanese_patient_enrollment.requirement: Clinical data in Japanese patients is generally required for J-NDA approval

scenarios.full_japanese_clinical_program: Required when high ethnic sensitivity is expected (e.g., drugs with narrow therapeutic index, CYP2D6/CYP2C19 substrates)
scenarios.bridging_study_only: Acceptable when ICH E5 criteria are met and ethnic sensitivity is low to moderate
scenarios.no_japanese_study: Rarely accepted; only when disease is extremely rare or foreign data is overwhelming and ethnic factors are minimal

japanese_patient_enrollment.minimum_enrollment: No fixed number specified; determined case-by-case based on statistical considerations for bridging
japanese_patient_enrollment.emphasis_on_pk: PK bridging in Japanese subjects is almost always required even when full efficacy bridging is waived

carcinogenicity_dose_selection_japan.japanese_definition: High dose causes no more than 10% body weight suppression compared to control, no poisoning deaths, and no serious toxicity findings (Toxicity Testing Guidelines)
carcinogenicity_dose_selection_japan.contrast_with_us: US traditionally used MTD (Maximum Tolerated Dose) exclusively
carcinogenicity_dose_selection_japan.contrast_with_eu: EU CPMP allowed 10% body weight suppression or mild organ toxicity
carcinogenicity_dose_selection_japan.harmonized_position: ICH S1C agreed on MTD with supplementary pharmacokinetic metric (minimum AUC ratio of 25x)

j_nda_format.description: Japanese New Drug Application follows CTD format with Japan-specific Module 1

content: Japanese labeling (package insert in Japanese format)

biomarker_qualification_submission.framework: ICH E16 implemented via MHLW Notification (January 20, 2011, Heisei 23)
structure: Section 2: Overview - overall biomarker qualification assessment (CTD Module 2)
Section 3: Quality - analytical method, manufacturing, quality characteristics (CTD Module 3)
Section 4: Nonclinical reports - method development, validation, in-vivo/in-vitro (CTD Module 4)
Section 5: Clinical reports - method development, validation, clinical trial reports (CTD Module 5)
biomarker_qualification_submission.japan_unique: Simultaneous submission to relevant regulatory authorities across ICH regions is recommended for biomarker qualification

re_examination_system.description: Japan-unique post-approval surveillance period during which generic competition is prohibited
re_examination_system.purpose: Collect real-world safety and efficacy data in Japanese population

jp18_pharmacopoeia.legal_basis: Article 41 of PMD Act (formerly Pharmaceutical Affairs Law, Showa 35 Law No. 145)
transition_provisions: Products in old JP: can use old names/standards until December 31, Reiwa 4
Products in new JP but not old: can be considered non-JP until December 31, Reiwa 4
Japanese names: old JP names valid until June 30, Reiwa 6
General rules: previous practices allowed until June 30, Reiwa 6

general_tests.section_2: Physical test methods - chromatography (2.01-2.05), spectroscopy (2.21-2.26), other physical (2.41-2.66)
general_tests.section_9: Reference standards, standard solutions, reagents, equipment (9.01-9.63)

structure.monographs_crude_drugs: Pages 1861-2086 (Herbal/crude drug monographs - UNIQUE to JP)

supplementary_info.G0: Drug quality fundamentals, QbD, QRM, PQS terminology
supplementary_info.G1: Analytical validation, system suitability, NIR spectroscopy
supplementary_info.G3: Biologics-related (peptide mapping, mass analysis, glycan analysis, electrophoresis, SPR, ELISA, virus safety, mycoplasma)
supplementary_info.G4: Microbiology (non-sterile quality, endotoxin alternatives, rapid methods, sterilization)
supplementary_info.G5: Crude drug related (botanical nomenclature, aristolochic acid, NMR quantitation)
supplementary_info.G6: Preparations (content uniformity, dissolution apparatus calibration, inhaler testing, tablet hardness)
supplementary_info.G7: Container/packaging (glass, plastic, rubber, moisture permeability, package integrity)
supplementary_info.GZ: Other (water for testing, pharmaceutical water quality, international harmonization)

key_differences_from_usp_ep.detail: JP contains extensive crude drug monographs (pages 1861-2086) for traditional Japanese/Chinese herbal medicines not found in USP or EP
key_differences_from_usp_ep.impact: Manufacturers of products containing crude drug components must comply with JP-specific standards

key_differences_from_usp_ep.detail: JP18 Section G3 includes specialized biologics methods: peptide mapping (G3-3), glycan profiling (G3-5), isoelectric focusing (G3-6), capillary electrophoresis (G3-7), SDS-PAGE (G3-8), host cell protein (G3-9), SPR (G3-10)
key_differences_from_usp_ep.impact: Biologics characterization must follow JP-specific protocols in addition to ICH Q6B

key_differences_from_usp_ep.area: Insoluble Particulate Testing for Protein Drugs
key_differences_from_usp_ep.detail: JP18 Section 6.17 provides dedicated test method for insoluble particulates in protein drug injections, separate from general injectable particulate testing (6.07)
key_differences_from_usp_ep.impact: Additional testing burden for protein/biologic injectables vs USP <787>/<788>

key_differences_from_usp_ep.detail: JP18 Section 6.10 dissolution methods may specify different apparatus or conditions than USP <711>
key_differences_from_usp_ep.impact: Dissolution specifications may need to be developed separately for JP compliance

key_differences_from_usp_ep.detail: JP18 Section 6.09 may differ in procedural details from USP <701>
JP18 Section 2.66 covers elemental impurities; harmonization with ICH Q3D is ongoing but JP implementation may differ in specifics
key_differences_from_usp_ep.impact: Check JP-specific limits vs ICH Q3D

key_differences_from_usp_ep.detail: JP18 Section 7 has specific tests for glass containers (7.01), plastic containers (7.02), and rubber closures (7.03) that may differ from USP <660>/<661>/<381>
key_differences_from_usp_ep.impact: Container/closure qualification may require JP-specific testing

key_differences_from_usp_ep.detail: JP18 GZ-1 and GZ-2 define pharmaceutical water standards that may have different specifications than USP <1231>
key_differences_from_usp_ep.impact: Water systems for JP-market manufacturing must meet JP standards

key_differences_from_usp_ep.detail: JP18 GZ-3 explicitly documents the state of international harmonization, identifying which JP tests are harmonized with USP/EP and which diverge
key_differences_from_usp_ep.impact: Provides roadmap for identifying JP-unique requirements

drug_interaction_guidelines.source: MHLW Guideline on Drug Interaction for Drug Development and Appropriate Provision of Information (February 8, 2019, Heisei 31)
drug_interaction_guidelines.scope: In vitro studies + clinical drug interaction studies during drug development + post-marketing information provision

key_japan_specific_elements.detail: Japan requires evaluation of a broader panel of transporters than FDA: P-gp, BCRP, OATP1B1, OATP1B3, OAT1, OAT3, OCT1, OCT2, MATE1, MATE2-K, MRP2, MRP4, BSEP
key_japan_specific_elements.fda_comparison: FDA guidance focuses on P-gp, BCRP, OATP1B1, OATP1B3, OAT1, OAT3, OCT2, MATE1, MATE2-K

key_japan_specific_elements.detail: Requires evaluation of CYP1A2, CYP2B6, CYP2C8, CYP2C9, CYP2C19, CYP2D6, CYP3A4, CYP3A5, and consideration of CYP2A6, CYP2E1, CYP2J2, CYP4F2
key_japan_specific_elements.note: CYP2C19 polymorphism is particularly relevant for Japanese population (higher poor metabolizer frequency than Caucasians)

key_japan_specific_elements.detail: Explicitly states that when simulation results are used for NDA, justification of model assumptions and validation of reliability from physiological, medical, pharmaceutical, and statistical viewpoints is required
Section 8 mandates specific DDI information in drug package inserts: Precautions section (8.1), Drugs in INTERACTIONS section (8.2), PHARMACOKINETICS section (8.3)
key_japan_specific_elements.japan_unique: Japanese package insert format differs from FDA PI and EMA SmPC

key_japan_specific_elements.detail: Specifically calls out grapefruit juice CYP3A inhibition and requires food interaction evaluation using final formulation
key_japan_specific_elements.note: Includes consideration of pediatric-specific food interactions (e.g., milk in neonates)

key_japan_specific_elements.detail: When contribution ratio (CR) of a metabolic pathway is estimated at 25% or more, clinical DDI studies with index drugs affecting that enzyme should be considered
Explicitly references the sorivudine/5-FU fatal interaction as precedent for evaluating non-P450 enzyme interactions, reflecting a historically important Japanese drug safety event

incentives.detail: PMDA provides research subsidies for designated orphan drug/device development
15% of eligible R&D expenses (minus PMDA grant amount) added to incremental R&D tax credit limit
Priority review and priority pre-application consultation
Up to 10 years for drugs, up to 7 years for devices (vs standard 8 years)
PMDA provides guidance on study planning, conduct, and submission preparation
